message:
fix(share): make scrub pseudonym salt deterministic for reproducible snapshots

The scrub snapshot pseudonymization used a salt derived from
`SystemTime::now()` and `std::process::id()`, meaning every invocation
produced different pseudonyms for the same input data. This made it
impossible to diff two snapshots taken at different times or to write
deterministic tests against scrubbed output.

Replace the non-deterministic hash-based salt with the preset name
itself (e.g., "default", "minimal"). This matches the Python reference
implementation's behavior where the salt is stable across runs for the
same preset, making snapshots reproducible: the same agent names and
message IDs always map to the same pseudonyms when using the same
preset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
